title: hirculus ranunculoides
intro: Hirculus ranunculoides, also known as the lesser celandine or fig buttercup, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Hirculus ranunculoides include its ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Hirculus ranunculoides include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
